entities.name: bhava
entities.cite: The bhavas, things, are vivrtta avibhagat

entities.name: śruti
entities.cite: the naming in sruti is as in a dream

entities.name: abhikhyā
entities.cite: Their abhikhya, their naming-and-appearing in sruti

entities.name: smṛti
entities.cite: smrti, however, is framed from the indicatory marks

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: The bhavas, things, are vivrtta avibhagat — unrolled out of the undivided

axioms.name: abhikhya
axioms.cite: Their abhikhya, their naming-and-appearing in sruti, is svapnavat: *as in a dream*

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: Their abhikhya, their naming-and-appearing in sruti, is svapnavat: *as in a dream*

axioms.name: immediate
axioms.cite: dream-presentation is immediate, involuntary, whole — the dreamer does not compose the dream but undergoes its self-showing; so the rsi does not construct the Veda but receives

axioms.name: vihita
axioms.cite: Smrti by contrast is work: bhava-tattvam vijnaya, having *understood* the reality, lingebhyah vihita — framed from marks, inferentially, deliberately (1.7 verbatim). Revelation is thus dream-like showing; tradition-literature is waking construction upon it

axioms.name: requires
axioms.cite: bhava-tattvam vijnaya, having *understood* the reality, lingebhyah vihita — framed from marks

axioms.name: deliberate
axioms.cite: Smrti by contrast is work: bhava-tattvam vijnaya, having *understood* the reality, lingebhyah vihita — framed from marks, inferentially, deliberately (1.7 verbatim)

axioms.name: unconstructed
axioms.cite: The pairing dignifies both: sruti's authority is its unconstructedness, smrti's is its method

denials.name: false_knowledge
denials.cite: The comparison is precise, not deflationary: dream-presentation is immediate, involuntary, whole

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: Of the things unrolled out of the undivided

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: the naming in sruti is as in a dream

claims.name: immediate
claims.cite: the naming in sruti is as in a dream

claims.name: vihita
claims.cite: is framed from the indicatory marks

claims.name: deliberate
claims.cite: after knowing the reality of things

claims.name: requires
claims.cite: after knowing the reality of things

rejected_readings.label: dream_illusionism
rejected_readings.rendering: Since sruti presents things in a dream-like manner, sruti's teachings are illusory and lack true authority.
rejected_readings.why: Misreads svapnavat (dream-like mode of presentation) as implying the non-reality of what is presented, when the commentary explicitly dignifies the immediacy and unconstructedness of sruti's showing. The denial of false_knowledge on sruti blocks this reading.

rejected_readings.label: smrti_superiority
rejected_readings.rendering: Smrti, being constructed through understanding and inference, is more reliable and authoritative than the dream-like presentation of sruti.
rejected_readings.why: Flattens the verse's careful distinction into a hierarchy; the verse dignifies both modes — sruti's authority lies in its unconstructedness and immediacy, smrti's in its method. The verse says both are necessary, not that one grades the other.

rejected_readings.label: constructed_sruti
rejected_readings.rendering: Sruti, like smrti, is a deliberate construction from marks and inferences about reality.
rejected_readings.why: Erases the fundamental distinction the verse establishes: sruti is immediate and unconstructed, while smrti is deliberate and inferential. This sort error (treating sruti as deliberate when it is predicated as immediate) makes the entire epistemological framework collapse.

notes: The verse establishes two epistemological modes: sruti's dream-like showing (immediate, whole, received) and smrti's deliberate construction (understood, marked, inferred). The challenge is that 'dream-like' (svapnavat) could be read either as (1) characterizing the phenomenology of reception (the commentary's reading), or (2) implying illusory content (the contested reading). The contract follows the commentary's disambiguation, preserving svapna-sakti as a power of presentation, not truth-negation. The sort assignments treat bhava-tattva as cognition (the act of understanding) rather than as a manifestation or absolute, capturing its epistemological function in the sanskrit-epistemic framework.
